skill.name: planning-with-files
skill.description: Implements Manus-style file-based planning for complex tasks. Creates task_plan.md, findings.md, and progress.md files to maintain working memory across long sessions.
skill.summary: File-based planning system inspired by Manus AI that uses persistent markdown files as external memory for complex multi-step tasks.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The shell command patterns detected are from markdown documentation code blocks and legitimate bash helper scripts that only perform file operations. No cryptographic code, network exfiltration, or credential access exists. This is a safe productivity skill for planning complex tasks using persistent markdown files.

content.user_title: Plan Complex Tasks with Persistent Files
content.value_statement: Long AI sessions cause context loss and forgotten goals. This skill uses markdown files as external memory to maintain task state across unlimited tool calls.
actual_capabilities: Creates structured task_plan.md files to track phases and progress
Maintains findings.md for research discoveries and technical decisions
Logs session activity in progress.md with timestamps and test results
Implements 3-strike error protocol to prevent repeating failed actions
Provides shell scripts for session initialization and completion checking
Auto-activates for complex tasks via session hooks
limitations: Requires manual file creation in project directory for first use
Templates must be copied from skill directory before use
Shell scripts require bash environment to execute
Does not automatically sync planning files across devices

use_cases.title: Multi-Step Development Projects
use_cases.description: Track complex coding tasks across 50+ tool calls with phase tracking, error logging, and decision documentation.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers building features or fixing bugs

use_cases.title: Research and Documentation
use_cases.description: Capture web search findings, browser results, and source materials in structured markdown before context is lost.

use_cases.title: System Exploration and Debugging
use_cases.description: Document discoveries, failed attempts, and resolutions when exploring unfamiliar codebases or debugging issues.

prompt_templates.title: Initialize Planning Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Create task_plan.md, findings.md, and progress.md files in my project directory. The goal is: [describe your task goal]. Start with Phase 1 for requirements gathering.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new complex task that will span multiple phases

prompt_templates.title: Track Bug Fix Progress
prompt_templates.prompt: Read task_plan.md and update the current phase status. Log the error encountered: [paste error]. Document the root cause and attempted fixes in the Errors Encountered table.
prompt_templates.scenario: Debugging an issue and need to track attempts and resolutions

prompt_templates.title: Capture Research Findings
prompt_templates.prompt: I just completed two web searches on [topic]. Update findings.md with the key discoveries, relevant URLs, and any technical decisions made based on this research.
prompt_templates.scenario: After gathering information from external sources

prompt_templates.title: Phase Completion and Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Mark Phase [X] as complete in task_plan.md. Summarize what was accomplished, files created or modified, and update progress.md with test results. Then set Phase [X+1] to in_progress.
prompt_templates.scenario: Completing a phase and transitioning to the next

output_examples.input: Initialize planning for a Python CLI todo app with add, list, and delete commands
output: Created task_plan.md with 5 phases: Requirements, Planning, Implementation, Testing, Delivery
Created findings.md with sections for requirements, research, and technical decisions
Created progress.md with session timestamp and initial status

output_examples.input: Log error: FileNotFoundError when reading config.json
output: Added to Errors Encountered table: FileNotFoundError, Attempt 1, Resolution: Create default config file
Updated progress.md error log with timestamp
Phase status remains in_progress until error resolved

best_practices: Create all three planning files before starting any complex task with 3+ steps
Read task_plan.md before making major decisions to keep goals in attention
Update findings.md immediately after every 2 view or browser operations
Log every error encountered, even if quickly resolved, to prevent repetition
Mark phase status changes explicitly: pending to in_progress to complete
anti_patterns: Starting execution without creating a task_plan.md file first
Silently retrying failed actions without logging the error
Repeating the same failing action instead of mutating approach
Creating planning files in the skill directory instead of project directory
Using TodoWrite for persistence instead of file-based storage

faq.question: Where should I create the planning files?
faq.answer: Create task_plan.md, findings.md, and progress.md in your project directory where you are working, not in the skill installation folder. This keeps planning files alongside your code.

faq.question: When should I update the planning files?
faq.answer: Update task_plan.md after each phase completes. Update findings.md after every 2 view or browser operations. Update progress.md throughout the session as actions are completed.

faq.question: What is the 2-Action Rule?
faq.answer: After every 2 view, browser, or search operations, immediately write key findings to findings.md. This prevents visual or multimodal information from being lost when context resets.

faq.question: How do I handle errors with this skill?
faq.answer: Log every error in the Errors Encountered table with attempt number and resolution. Never repeat the same failing action. After 3 failed attempts, escalate to the user with what you tried.

faq.question: Can I use this for simple tasks?
faq.answer: For simple questions, single-file edits, or quick lookups, skip the planning files. Use this pattern for tasks with 3+ steps, research projects, or anything requiring organization across many tool calls.

faq.question: What are the shell scripts for?
faq.answer: init-session.sh creates all three planning files with template content. check-complete.sh verifies all phases in task_plan.md are marked complete. Both are optional helpers for automation.
